response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a capable full-stack software developer with a long career spanning web, mobile, and embedded systems, but her profile has no meaningful overlap with the core technical requirements of a Principal Performance Architect for AI accelerator hardware. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, deep-learning workload analysis, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, or hardware-software co-design anywhere in her history. Her closest adjacent work — low-latency DLang micro-services at Dunnhumby and AI-agent tooling at re:cinq — is architecturally distant from silicon-level performance modeling. The educational background (two bachelor's degrees, no master's or PhD) also falls below the preferred academic profile for a principal-level IC in this highly specialized domain.
strengths: Demonstrated low-latency and performance-sensitive engineering experience (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput micro-services at Dunnhumby)
Broad multi-stack breadth across embedded, systems, and web engineering showing strong foundational adaptability
Active open-source contributor and technical educator, indicating collaborative and knowledge-sharing disposition valued at principal level
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator design — the core technical pillars of the role
No background in deep-learning training or inference optimization; AI-adjacent work is limited to agent infrastructure tooling, not hardware workload analysis
Educational profile (two bachelor's degrees, no advanced degree) and overall skill set are firmly aligned to application software, not semiconductor or hardware performance engineering
